summary: Excellent soundboard transfer of a short but lively 1969 show with standout Pigpen vocals on Good Morning Little Schoolgirl, Hard to Handle and an epic Lovelight
review: The Charlie Miller‑sourced soundboard capture is top‑shelf – clear, well‑balanced and praised as “excellent”. Musically the set is brief yet energetic, highlighted by Jerry’s slide on Hard to Handle, Pigpen’s fiery Schoolgirl performance, a jam‑heavy Doin’ That Rag, a soulful Morning Dew and a marathon 24‑minute Lovelight. The only real blemish noted is the infamous ugly yellow dog joke.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show highly (4–5 stars) and praise the superb soundboard transfer; weighted average of strong show quality (≈4) and excellent recording quality (≈5) yields ~4.3.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Transfer by Charlie Miller described as top‑shelf, crisp and clean; no issues reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A short, roughly one‑hour set that feels incomplete (no second set present). Despite its brevity the performance is lively and well‑played, with Pigpen dominating vocals.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Plays slide on Hard to Handle; emphasizes a dramatic G at the end of Doin' That Rag; praised throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work noted as being at its very best during the jam sections.
band_member_comments.Drums: Both drummers are highlighted as being at their very best.
